description: Fix unguarded nested property access in Slack plugin tools that can crash with TypeError when the Slack API returns unexpected response shapes. The slackApi() function uses unchecked type casts on API responses, so tool handlers must defensively guard nested property access.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/slack && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Guard nested property access in set-channel-topic and set-channel-purpose handlers
userStories.description: As a plugin user, I want set_channel_topic and set_channel_purpose tools to return a descriptive ToolError instead of crashing with TypeError when the Slack API response is missing expected nested fields (channel.topic or channel.purpose).
acceptanceCriteria: set-channel-topic handle() guards access to data.channel and data.channel.topic — if either is nullish, values default safely (e.g., value falls back to params.topic, creator to '', last_set to 0) instead of throwing TypeError
set-channel-purpose handle() guards access to data.channel and data.channel.purpose — same defensive defaulting pattern
Both tools continue to return correct values when the API response is well-formed (no behavioral change for the happy path)
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Files: plugins/slack/src/tools/set-channel-topic.ts (lines 30-32) and plugins/slack/src/tools/set-channel-purpose.ts (lines 30-32).

The bug: Both handlers access `data.channel.topic.value` and `data.channel.purpose.value` without null checks. The `slackApi<T>()` function casts the API response to `T` without runtime validation, so if the Slack API ever returns a response without the nested `topic`/`purpose` object (e.g., an unexpected API change, a permission issue, or a partial response), accessing `.value` on undefined throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined`.

Fix: Use optional chaining with nullish coalescing for all nested accesses. For example:
```typescript
value: data.channel?.topic?.value ?? params.topic,
creator: data.channel?.topic?.creator ?? '',
last_set: data.channel?.topic?.last_set ?? 0,
```

Same pattern for set-channel-purpose. This matches the defensive coding style already used in other Slack tools (e.g., send-message.ts line 28: `data.channel ?? ''`).

userStories.title: Guard data.channel access in get-channel-info handler
userStories.description: As a plugin user, I want get_channel_info to handle a missing channel object in the API response gracefully instead of crashing with TypeError.
acceptanceCriteria: get-channel-info handle() guards access to data.channel before passing it to mapChannel() — if data.channel is nullish, it passes an empty object to mapChannel() so the tool returns safe defaults instead of crashing
Tool continues to return correct values when the API response is well-formed (no behavioral change for the happy path)
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/slack/src/tools/get-channel-info.ts (line 22).

The bug: `mapChannel(data.channel)` passes `data.channel` directly. The `slackApi<T>()` return type says `data.channel` is `SlackChannel`, but this is an unchecked cast. If the Slack API response doesn't include a `channel` field, `data.channel` is `undefined`, and `mapChannel(undefined)` crashes when it accesses `c.id` (TypeError).

Fix: Use nullish coalescing to provide a safe fallback:
```typescript
return { channel: mapChannel(data.channel ?? {}) };
```

The `mapChannel` function already accepts `Partial<SlackChannel>` and defaults all fields, so passing an empty object produces safe output. The type annotation on `data` may need a `?` on `channel` to make it explicit: `channel?: SlackChannel`.
